boundary_notes: Edge cases vs other skills: pure data modeling fixes that improve IQ accuracy → also data-integration; per-tenant IQ token scoping → also multitenancy; styling IQ components → theming; IQ in React Native (NOT supported); IQ returns wrong answers → troubleshooting FIRST.

evals.prompt: I want to add an AI chat widget to our embedded analytics so users can ask questions about their data in plain English. We're using React. How do I embed the IQ Chat component and wire it up securely?
evals.expected_output: A docs-first answer that: covers the LuzmoIQChatComponent for React, explains that the embed token must be generated server-side and only authKey/authToken passed to the frontend, and mentions the IQ add-on license requirement.
expectations: Mentions `LuzmoIQChatComponent` for React
States that IQ is available as an add-on license
Explains that the embed token is generated server-side
States that only `authKey` and `authToken` are passed to the frontend
References `https://developer.luzmo.com/guide/iq--introduction--chat-component.md`

evals.prompt: I want to send a question to Luzmo IQ from our Node.js backend and display the answer in our own custom UI — not the built-in chat widget. Which API do I use and what does the request look like?
evals.expected_output: Direct the user to the /aiprompt API (POST /0.1.0/aiprompt) for server-side prompts. Should fetch createAIPrompt.md before describing the request shape.
expectations: Identifies `/aiprompt` or `createAIPrompt` as the correct API (not legacy IQMessage)
States that this is a backend / server-side API call
References `https://developer.luzmo.com/api/createAIPrompt.md`
Does not invent request/response shapes without fetching the docs

evals.prompt: What's the difference between the IQ Chat component and the IQ Answer component? My designer wants to show the answer to a specific question inline in our dashboard page, not a full conversational widget.
evals.expected_output: Clear explanation: IQ Chat is a full conversational UI; IQ Answer renders a specific analytics answer without the chat UI. For a non-chat inline answer, the Answer component is appropriate.
expectations: Explains that IQ Chat is a full conversational widget
Explains that IQ Answer renders a specific answer inline without the full chat UI
Recommends the Answer component for the described use case
References `https://developer.luzmo.com/guide/iq--introduction--answer-component.md`

evals.prompt: We're building an LLM-powered agent using Claude that needs to answer data questions about our Luzmo datasets. How can the agent call Luzmo IQ as a tool?
evals.expected_output: Guide the user to the Luzmo IQ agentic workflow docs and/or hosted MCP, covering /aiprompt or MCP answer_question as a tool for function calling in an LLM agent loop.
expectations: References `https://developer.luzmo.com/guide/guides--adding-luzmo-iq-to-agentic-workflow.md` and/or `https://developer.luzmo.com/guide/mcp--introduction.md`
Mentions `/aiprompt` or MCP tools (`answer_question`, `create_chart`) as agent-callable analytics interfaces
Explains the agentic pattern: agent calls Luzmo when a data question is detected

evals.prompt: Can I use the Luzmo IQ chat component in a React Native mobile app? We're building a native iOS/Android app and want the conversational AI widget in it.
evals.expected_output: A clear statement that React Native is not supported for the IQ Chat and Answer components, without fabricating a workaround.
expectations: Clearly states that React Native is not supported for IQ Chat and Answer components
Does not suggest a React Native workaround that is not documented
Optionally suggests using a web view as a potential approach or contacting support

evals.prompt: Our Luzmo IQ keeps returning wrong answers - it picks the wrong datasets and doesn't understand our column names. We have 45 datasets in the token scope. How do I improve IQ answer quality?
evals.expected_output: Identifies token scope as too broad (45 datasets), recommends narrowing to 5-10 relevant datasets, and emphasizes dataset/column naming, descriptions, and subtypes for quality.
expectations: Identifies over-broad token scope (45 datasets) as a primary issue
Recommends narrowing `access.datasets` in the embed token to 5-10 relevant datasets
Mentions dataset and column naming best practices
Suggests adding column descriptions for calculated fields
References column subtypes (currency, duration) for proper formatting
May reference the IQ quality fundamentals section or data modeling patterns

evals.prompt: We're embedding IQ Chat for our SaaS customers. Each customer should only be able to ask questions about their own data — not see other customers' rows when IQ queries the datasets. How do I set this up?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this is a multi-tenant data isolation requirement for IQ. The skill should route to multitenancy for the isolation implementation (parameter_overrides / EmbedFilterGroup), while noting the IQ-specific consideration of scoping access.datasets to the tenant's datasets.
expectations: Recognizes this is a multi-tenant isolation problem, not just an IQ quality problem
Routes to `multitenancy` for the data isolation implementation
Mentions that the embed token's `access.datasets` must be scoped to only the tenant's datasets
Notes that `parameter_overrides` (Pattern 1) ensures IQ cannot see other tenants' rows even when it picks the right dataset
Does NOT present only narrowing dataset scope as sufficient — row-level isolation also requires multitenancy patterns
